textByHash.008b036797a5b5bb833a7ffa129c1035c04e360e: Weeellll... You know I told you that it was the Duke's birthday?
textByHash.05f095ca5a42976aac48a783f4dc2f7c036f5f35: I'm not entirely sure. What am I, an encyclopedia?
textByHash.05fd7d8be83fc9b521310bd44f4fb3043b5678fd: What did the half-wit say to the cook?
textByHash.086a019b1d9d01bfb399b4dd815906a1a621238e: I guess I don't have anything more to say to you then.
textByHash.0e5cdbd09862267d9ca30bbafa2960f2af3c44ac: My wife gets sea sick, and I have an irrational fear of eyepatches. I don't see it working myself.
textByHash.0fa70db8768a325de76db1a21941b95e5696b22a: Why do you think I work in a disreputable inn filled with rough scoundrels on the very edge of the Wilderness? I work here because I need the job!
textByHash.0ff657471ace3dff074b144b448f55a1e39634dd: With the foods, and that big portal thingy, and everything!
textByHash.10a799c81ef6acbb685760a1d83e296a37d4d6f0: After you managed to save all of the members of the secret council with those weird foods, the culinaromancer tried to make a run for it, but you followed him into his magic portal and Biff! Bang! Boom!
textByHash.116e977791adb614a43201ce41d108073d149e1a: No, not really. I think you might have mentioned the name Traiborn though.
textByHash.12cfdd302083f872d871f85c66fd1221df7058af: No, honestly, you MUST go in there!
textByHash.1324efb880bdeef52497e8ed4b3ffc8a4f2c355b: Yes, but then came the impressive part!
textByHash.1328e79e022cecf6901dee1874b0825bc3f62561: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?}}
textByHash.137409e1c8b256b9b6cf20a6c3373ef457ac79fa: Fishcakes? As in with fish eggs and fish milk?
textByHash.1521dab870307c7b5eccb773109f13f2596365b4: Oh no, it's terrible, isn't it? There was rock dust everywhere, it got on all my ingredients!
textByHash.1885f04732d6e9f19a50195896d7eeb888845efd: She said 'Can you tell me any good jokes?' Got ya! [laugh][laugh][laugh]!
textByHash.1a4abb4880b45db2fe0f41c125739442f5698f26: Ah, I don't know where they get them, they usually have them at herblore and magic shops.
textByHash.1aac2a9a1879d3a1b0a9b58a7139e36bee6c53e8: How dare you? I put a lot of effort into cleaning this kitchen. My daily sweat and elbow-grease keep this kitchen clean!
textByHash.1cc27526317616fe6f200cda3f06b1eb414b809d: Hmm, I don't think that's right.
textByHash.1dbb095fa0615a35d836b69adc8072e1403fef44: I don't know. A newt's face?
textByHash.1fc420173295dd607e341b711b3eeccd1e60f8aa: After that you told me that you sought out and killed the fearsome Evil Chicken, to get one of his eggs, which was then mixed with the other ingredients.
textByHash.216cb8808addc2b0e531c344ea2b55ce044178e1: What did you expect to hear? I suppose you expected me to tell you an inspiring story about how working here can teach you to find hope in the darkest places, or how to see the best in everything?
textByHash.21c7ed828247fe61e6e7cd1f609bbe7379ea5800: I used to be the cook for the old Duke of Lumbridge. Visiting dignitaries praised me for my fine banquets!
textByHash.21f9a084fe6df8e15dcb946ca9a87d4de4566b77: Ah yes! Why, I was so impressed with that recipe you created that I memorised it!
textByHash.260b9adac4305d6a57eda6b9dbe8841ecf729f11: In fact, it's a good thing I met you. It's the Duke's birthday today, and I should be making him a lovely big birthday cake. I need eggs, flour and milk...
textByHash.263e0582dc0880c611b6b808cc46ee030e2892e5: Finally you made some cinnamon by grinding the branch of a Dramen Tree and sprinkled it on top of the creme brulee.
textByHash.283ad781e5ea9f4ae36f0933a65e60b2a7b9a03f: I know I'm such a failure... I bring shame to my honourable ancestor, and now I need help again...
textByHash.2a19fb755e723adb1a515e0a71ffc2d306db6374: Well, as the official sponsored drink of Castle Wars it might be worth looking around there.
textByHash.2a47dd8f4a3aa1bcf253b3fff7455ee7d656f485: I am a real cook! I haven't got time to be chatting about Culinary Fashion. I am in desperate need of help!
textByHash.2a59c8217147c32321de3af230c50c748b2671da: Bah! I'm not letting it defeat me. Now, let me concentrate on my cooking.
textByHash.2b06ab0c76688b6fbeb60f383d2969672f7d42d9: Well come back when you have all of them! And hurry! It is a VERY important meeting!
textByHash.2e5e5c8c7a0d481fc13a5de8cb4404947272819e: Great! Just like before! I've been reading the recipe and I know how to make the fishcakes now.
textByHash.2f198c232d6c0c8a2cbb2e2c292693c501c0564c: Most of the ingredients I was able to obtain fairly easily, but there were some... oddities... that I have been unable to lay my hands on.
textByHash.3423206b05cc01aefd8d9ea9c24efdf3594659a1: Well, it was something that sounded ABSOLUTELY DISGUSTING that you picked up on Ape Atoll...
textByHash.360e4cf0ced1ea33980bd6324ec2f563aa02166f: (sniff) Weeeeelll... I thought that I would recreate the meal Franizzard cooked all of those years ago, the one that impressed
textByHash.36a4730c75469a2dc6d375676f92847bbff4c7bc: The dwarf? Oh, yeah, I think that was an easy one, didn't you say?
textByHash.37af831624cc7a1a131d86ea600e5b5878880185: No, that's the worst thing I could do. I'd get in terrible trouble.
textByHash.39dbb708c00978fbb0d25a3f4de3ffd60b39759e: Apparently, a creme brulee supreme needs to be flambed at exactly the right temperature, and the only fire that is hot enough to do this is the breath of a dragon. Luckily, while killing black dragons near the Evil
textByHash.3b9efa059d061ef64c6c163244e59873d086f4aa: You absolutely MUST go through into the dining room just there and see the feast I have prepared.
textByHash.3cc877c3c605c554575203e69c9d3b52c30fe407: He said 'Can you tell me any good jokes?' Got ya! [laugh][laugh][laugh]!
textByHash.3d23f5ef538442e2aadc4b5368ae31da7c3fe7e9: Talk to Millie, she'll help, she's a lovely girl and a fine Miller. Make sure you take a pot with you for the flour though, you've got one on you already.
textByHash.3f516ce1aa1f091c94f0e6f43522f5fc12c4868f: Talk to Millie, she'll help, she's a lovely girl and a fine Miller. Make sure you take a pot with you for the flour though, there should be one on the table in here.
textByHash.44981a67bbd8a65d3301990af1dbb5cc042f44b3: Well, apparently after you have done the above then by adding ashes you create a unique new drink.
textByHash.475f7d0c3cd112fcfb6e49af05c1cb665d69133f: Why haven't you gone and looked at the feast I have prepared yet? Trust me, it will be a sight the likes of which you will never see again!
textByHash.48fd87ae839ba3540ff1c28ac3311b615d1326b3: Well thank you! Hand them over, please.
textByHash.49094cb4ac16b1ad7490ee8c73b8e228fe7fbeca: Why should I give you free food?
textByHash.4ad3bebe8782a74a1c6f36d2104f02e03c6d32e7: I suppose I could sell you some cabbage, if you're willing to pay for it. Cabbage is good for you.
textByHash.4beb6c547fcf0d247bebc61c0e54851b606d122b: Can you give me any free money?
textByHash.4bf54b92b31adcb4a7d777281bd9c32601576e33: This big important secret meeting that Franizzard cooked for is held here in Lumbridge castle every 10 years, and tonight will be the tenth anniversary of it!
textByHash.4c8be6d92343909946cfabe4c4a9252872f2a31b: Bah! People these days only appreciate junk food.
textByHash.4cef87064f55192ce228467dbe5e7b1f7888ae7b: Overnight I was fired and replaced by some fool who can't even bake a cake without help!
textByHash.4dc0df6ba87cdf725da24e9902cf2d5971a9c1e9: There is a Mill fairly close, go North and then West. Mill Lane Mill is just off the road to Draynor. I usually get my flour from there.
textByHash.4ea6b0a16b5e0e09eb2db4c220848e49b376edda: Well, they're not exactly exotic, they're just the basic ingredients of a cake.
textByHash.4eb38445c2bc39f4d023039ac49ed22d2298c395: Did you ever know that you're my hero?
textByHash.4ef9544e59dee52e0c663a4ed78908a2931d83d8: Oooohhh... I really hope you do...
textByHash.4ff290000513af457a3c1d274985a6d24565d7fb: Well, Murphy used to bring in Giant Crabs occasionally. He hasn't had any of them in for a while though.
textByHash.51f8919ed4da1808ebf5c91084c359e44e97ca03: No chance mate, it's the principle. I won't make food for such a low class criminal...
textByHash.54e8df0f7ab076a70c88dd96db152aabc9719664: Well, that was a weird one alright, you told me that they couldn't agree on what food they liked, so you ended up just mushing a bunch of stuff together somehow which seemed to do the trick.
textByHash.57a81b0c7471a8c42edc00443ca45f465396e16f: Well, you're obviously trustworthy, I guess I could tell you...
textByHash.584b69732e84d3620249581cdd5ad0cb846a224e: One hundred years ago there was a very important meeting held here in Lumbridge Castle, and he cooked a banquet for the meeting, which was so stupendously amazing that he was offered a job for life, along with all
textByHash.58ccb51cd89be1be3fbe746f186fbf96db6cd7ad: Ah, so you've heard of it then? But for this recipe I'd need a fresh one; you can't just buy me one from a shop or from your friends.
textByHash.594e3e94e44e2f5b450535291e132fc3ec14c335: Well, he was a great chef, and also my ancestor.
textByHash.5c37418193f8580320356bac636dcf6753ea2a24: Bear in mind it will only work with very fresh ingredients, so you will have to make your own cocktail, not rely on one of the pre made ones Blurberry sells.
textByHash.5d3e27a549d8f85afaa0053de0f0d8d745d02447: It isn't. I don't even know when his birthday is!
textByHash.5d8653088f5a9de934460e75506934f42db13e28: I've always wondered how many cannons the castle has. Could you find out for me?
textByHash.5e419764914e2cd5d4eb59da65276fea1c55e0e0: Hmm... we don't get many food orders in 'ere. Last person to order one of them was barred for thievin'.
textByHash.5ef2c633c979740855f3c3c7fcbfc4f75a96d5eb: Let me take a look at it.
textByHash.5f13c597ccbb4086914575f911e3de6679393a66: Why are you asking me to sell you food if you haven't got any money? Go away!
textByHash.5f7d25bdbcfcf51e787ea6bb74980c362b5fbbf6: You had best go and have a word with him to see if he can tell you what happened.
textByHash.605a077d7a666af107102428c2e2a467431c4ed6: Oh, you told me that that crazy wizard in the wizards' tower helped you with that.
textByHash.613877d282ded821cb3f9b1713e402e5e40f8a6a: I need the following ingredients as soon as possible!
textByHash.615e8c7d5e0bbb63ffa89416776ebb0305723ea1: Please get the ingredients quickly - I'm running out of time! The Duke will throw me into the streets!
textByHash.62461b9677b73501602a23b719493df9cf666fca: Take this to him, it should cover the cost of the repair. You should be able to find him in his shop to the south.
textByHash.628cd6027fdc4d962b35ff4736ae17bf8a78e088: How is the adventuring going, my friend?
textByHash.63031e8178b14a7a4f273aca0b7678456c192b1a: Well, er, pleased to meet you, Zanik.
textByHash.66dda4e15ba75b06a3ad89b06666c96b733f21fa: I'm just glad you were around, who knows WHAT could have happened if you weren't able to help us!
textByHash.67fdfe97a947f8d56faac3d263926a2fb1d0fa23: It's pretty simple actually. All you have to do is use the ingredients on each other and then cook the fishcakes on a range.
textByHash.68d6c9cd77cddb65d2e39436b2b6fd4b41afde8b: Wow! You were incredible! The way you defeated the Culinaromancer, you made it seem so easy!
textByHash.6b633099954e26ac4223d0e1aec54052ca4206b3: Well, they are common enough ingredients, although I must admit the recipe I am following is very strange indeed...
textByHash.6cdb720a182549e3c25377612f7325e0a159c9c4: But then someone found a rule that said that only one family could hold that post.
textByHash.70588700642d1bbae3c383bf18833c8c2f3ec8b8: I have given you 100 coins to cover any expenses you may incur, feel free to keep the change.
textByHash.72bb446894f4ee960d49675fc0aba523f2885a6f: Then, you take some bread. I recommend making it fresh, but to each their own. Slice it in half and slide the beautifully cooked beef between the two halves...
textByHash.745b955ef7ea3b8cb02f20807f17e58f849eb7ec: Oh yes. You were quite adamant about it. Specifically black dragons. Specifically near the Evil Chicken's lair.
textByHash.76e2b7a3cac51ee9d81a7a5a02b3d0ae5e1a813e: I needed to test you and see if I could trust you...
textByHash.78a19cd93741cee74af0730b2923f8e84685d7d4: Ooooh dear, I might have, but it's all very embarrassing...
textByHash.795039cbe05bf399447068c187fbc1afc185e0c9: Yes, do us both a favour.
textByHash.79e4992e0f707e99a687f162ced093cce95f1084: Alright, sir. I can start work immediately.
textByHash.7a1bf883f519c39935070ac103b29d2c78febfd6: Like it? It's sucked the warmth and humanity out of me, and I've become a hollow shell holding nothing but anger, misery and gourmet recipes.
textByHash.7a8db960655b025d87de1bc66c816ee7b4fbf1a0: Oh, you told me you used sweetcorn in a mill!
textByHash.7b7777348fa3fc39f08eda9fc895958c47de09b6: Of course I do! It was amazing!
textByHash.7e3c69d8dff45cc3715b092ebd54ce8835877c62: The pirate? Yes, you used fishcakes didn't you?
textByHash.7fcaf3a4aa43e35c179b51a0364006a776be8ddd: Yeah. Pre-arranged combats, caught thieves on show, that sort of thing.
textByHash.8023ea8ea78fb02cd17763a839e977976d1a2b2f: Well, a 'Dirty Blast' is a freshly made Fruit Blast, but then for some reason you add ash at the end!
textByHash.802de76f536c09955808cc52342753b0e5f3bb1e: Are you having a laugh? No cash up front, no cook.
textByHash.80853782a834bb76349159de95015715cb953a0f: [sniff] Don't talk to me about cakes...
textByHash.81f560ee1cf44c4c1d6b7418d56bd8422aa028de: Please can you help me? The feast is scheduled to start very soon, and I simply cannot leave!
textByHash.830df197b0cfca61acab2deb1e5907d12fd001ff: Why are you asking me to sell you food if you can't carry it? Go away!
textByHash.86713a5f0c50da4bd6dcaa13bec96e56986c2bee: Oh thank you, thank you. I need milk, an egg and flour. I'd be very grateful if you can get them for me.
textByHash.89c3250735c6c2ad62dabacf5fae0147e49c6f2f: sorted him out once and for all!
textByHash.8cc009a3aa480c846a8a8ec93f2ee41625931638: Did you ever hear of Franizzard Van Lumbcook?
textByHash.8df4c12a9e14b45b10143ea71b717e1d382608ea: Hey, who said you could use that?
textByHash.8f5a2a098182a6aeb792dde13dfc48b3e478af65: I knew it! I'm not making another meal for that thievin' git! Clear off.
textByHash.93a680ffb69ee2a28b555957dc8d5acf95d2395a: There is a cattle field on the other side of the river, just across the road from the Groats' Farm.
textByHash.94c2eb202e133d748b37f06f17889e10c22bdf70: But any chicken should lay eggs.
textByHash.99b5fa7b5df548e0d2067bab28070180da4a92ca: I'm sure you did. It'll be enough for Marley, anyway.
textByHash.9c51d12a84afc192f8282d5dcea73d977867402a: I agree, but it's what the recipe specifically needs.
textByHash.9d851eacde45b620097f65ed4291200a063442c6: I distinctly remember you saying you were going to stop talking to me. Yet here you are again, yakking on and on in my earhole when I'm trying to cook! What do you want now?
textByHash.9ebd26f24abcd399e5f447e0bdecb4b63f046ffd: I know what it's like, not knowin' where your next meals going to come from, so I let him eat 'ere for free.
textByHash.9fd14dbc3d5e9bb59822c382845dcf6c32810758: Basically, what you do is juice a pineapple, an orange and a lemon, then put them all into a cocktail shaker.
textByHash.a06adb8f577d4a25f2a8f85d7890fa2fb29cca0c: But to do that... (sigh) No, it just won't work...
textByHash.a11c8f8602fb4b4fc346cda7f3d76816aaafddf2: Go ahead - it's a very good range. It's easier to use than most other ranges.
textByHash.a2546178e121d72f7183bc620040e3212f885735: For Saradomin's sake! Take some bread, cut it in half and stick some cooked Steak inside, done.
textByHash.a2bab541d68caa7ecf599147c327bf612b0ea52c: Well, I suppose if I give you the recipe, you can make it yourself.
textByHash.a35a67bc4d36c407a0004db3085ada6de2ed3d39: Ah well, at least you keep coming back to life!
textByHash.a3a13654b59b097d5986263ceb3f80eb1fe29588: No, I'm not. The world is caving in around me - I am overcome by dark feelings of impending doom.
textByHash.a48d15e9634436848ae2f53b89ae2818d0a462e0: Pour that into a glass, then top with a slice of lemon.
textByHash.a77c8d3e6ee841de3a95d0b6a2b4e565d7302c75: Certainly. Everyone knows that Skrach loves Jubbly Chompy, preferably roasted on a spit! The bird's carcass is so big, it can't be cooked any other way.
textByHash.a9c738806af66c24d56d23b092920204a4f63917: Err thank you. It's a pretty ordinary cooks hat really.
textByHash.ab3607fcdb1243e20e57f4ffdc20e1e37dd82b53: And hey presto, you've got yourself a delicious, perfectly made Steak sandwich.
textByHash.abc456b79f36f4b122146ed6ed09e2bf6679ef1c: Please tell me how many cannons there are.
textByHash.ac8af4e0f1fcd2ccc6b8904e6041f4741789b768: But of course! It was an unusual dish, an unusual dish indeed! First you blend together, in a pot, some milk and some cream. Then add cornflour to the mixture.
textByHash.ad1ea01a748525440a8843f320b889ecada08cc9: Yes. I thought that there was a page missing from the cookbook because it seemed so easy, but it turns out it really IS that easy!
textByHash.ae479777eb1d1c0227bee6fef559365d18e900a9: You'll need an empty bucket for the milk itself. I do see you've got a bucket with you already luckily!
textByHash.af7cda46c6138fe98f57a4ba8a95e445f3acbde8: It was worth a try. Anyway...
textByHash.b12ad9ece4ca36929e3ffd4e44aa98041d3a3d91: I want to prove to the world that I am a great cook too!
textByHash.b2b491b1e26584b94000fb22454f6bb0f2142a49: Watch your mouth! Making a Steak sandwich is a delicate art!
textByHash.b2c5a8d07482bae498fa894438792106bc8866a0: I'm sorry? How do you mean?
textByHash.b4a8c6bdaf74281e15faa57037a774a9a25d1022: That's an odd coincidence... Were you planning on making a cake too?
textByHash.b5230da3c71305a1749e1b55b1300c5fe39dbaa8: Well, I have an incredibly important banquet that I have to prepare for, and I am using an ancient cookbook to recreate the meal my famous ancestor made a hundred years ago.
textByHash.b5500d487f4fa907a0fcf11b76e82e0f0a3557d8: And a glass of greenman's ale.
textByHash.b588ef5f6b086dfeb579d961ea97601321f8ae5a: What, er, who is this? Is it a cave goblin?
textByHash.b59a4e706c976c0b106987d313c22a3ea14ac081: Yeah, I didn't understand it either, but you mentioned something about demonic incursions and evil cats too!
textByHash.b613bfa8eadcabe065188443e9754ff4f37404ad: Of course! Which bit can't you remember doing?
textByHash.b705e97fad2200ae8139dab2ceb9b2ba1fb86042: There are apparently some kelp patches off the coast near Rimmington. Murphy knows more about that.
textByHash.b7955d6527ef6fbec4d947223d48a7ae6b8d2028: everybody so much... There's just this small problem though...
textByHash.b7acbb63e830208717e0b7ce5bad3868028eab79: Thanks for the ingredients you have got so far. Please get the rest quickly - I'm running out of time! The Duke will throw me into the streets!
textByHash.b7f70224e2c94a5f8ee03bdc3c54e85a3231a72c: I wouldn't know much about that, but I believe you talked to Rantz quite a bit over in the Feldip hills. Apparently, he got you to do him quite a few favours before you actually got the Jubbly.
textByHash.b84786a4bec03a4625c883518246bd6a07f569eb: Oh dear, oh dear, oh dear, I'm in a terrible terrible mess! It's the Duke's birthday today, and I should be making him a lovely big birthday cake.
textByHash.b9e27e820c984cd28900e29e2b206e99a702004a: Alright, madam. I can start work immediately.
textByHash.ba899c56607043cd75ba31c944f884c07b02485a: Well, that's what the salesman told us anyway...
textByHash.bb4782459889815dde8ead551ea916bb2f356376: One Eye Of Newt A freshly made 'Dirty Blast' A rotten tomato
textByHash.bbd969c421f36d78d47258f90d7f443d65df1797: Then you travelled to the Kharazi Jungle and searched the plants there until you found a vanilla stick, which was then added to the mixture.
textByHash.bc6d601bca3bcf5a9e36669bb32f152be52b4d75: It's harder to make than it sounds! Now get out before I whack you one for being so rude.
textByHash.bcac45090ba3885bc7f73ac7c517b214c3d5414e: It's a deal. Now, make sure you eat it all up. Cabbage is good for you.
textByHash.bfd3fcdcfc984b5faf02c834392931ab815c81cb: You've brought me everything I need! I am saved! Thank you!
textByHash.c0894114f8c659e7bb1ca4481cd8946d2c5e5929: I normally get my eggs from the Groats' farm, on the other side of the river.
textByHash.c0a43bebb8b02001f40fe7370ad3452dc0d7cf41: I will pay you upfront for any expenses you may incur while obtaining them, and of course you may keep the change for being so helpful to me.
textByHash.c4afe5f6f4f063fd7cb56fc45eb0507d66a466b4: Murphy also used to bring in Giant Crabs occasionally. He hasn't had any of them in for a while though.
textByHash.c5a3e775edeea30dd8c5cd8ce83d9a062b79862e: Yes, I'd imagine he can! Not to mention, he owes me one!
textByHash.c704e095b7a08b7407ba0c68ad55b0eda08ab7d7: Oh, and there are apparently some kelp patches off the coast near Rimmington. Murphy knows about that too.
textByHash.c9031f074317ff80404a65f8a5ad7ba1c6e59ac4: Maybe, but I won't be holding my breath.
textByHash.cae151644470cfdada8a160c5bafe0c498aaa09a: What do you want? I'm a little busy here, so make it quick!
textByHash.cb48242e930f6bdf0bb125ce0743c382957b9e33: I'm not entirely sure, but I hear they're sold for public spectacles.
textByHash.cce30d279b7be75fef1b90dea5dd87dcf2cdf3a2: Hmph! I don't work for just anyone, you know! I refuse to work for someone who's below level 30 Construction!
textByHash.cef601c40deaa4fcb9f81ab65465d1393920e325: You jumped in right behind him and fought him to the death!
textByHash.d3796016964b88b19cc073e42263b5a0b61646d1: Oh yeah! I didn't understand that one myself, but apparently you went and asked him how he likes his stew, then made him remember it after he'd tasted it!
textByHash.d46bb4d9c29df34b582c9d2ac8d192a9be460531: I am the finest cook in all Gielinor! I can also make good time going to the bank or the sawmill.
textByHash.d4d123ddd187c5528324220223055f1ae1be7b92: As for the grinding part, all you have to do is use a pestle and mortar on it, that ought to get it sorted.
textByHash.d96cdc1fa204627af429091d2cb78ecf3e56099e: Yes, I must admit I had never heard of it either until reading through my ancestor's cookbook.
textByHash.da106315a33516d03d31bbd389a5efe9aaecac6b: Oh nooooo... What am I going to do now?
textByHash.da226ab74f2a2c7424a307d578bb0916d154152b: Talk to Gillie Groats, she looks after the Dairy cows - she'll tell you everything you need to know about milking cows!
textByHash.dad9931f440913b1f1316fe297e07410a4de5a9d: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?
textByHash.db4513d1e94de6f2ff09da1855f18ae5ac14e7e7: Apparently you need Ground Cod, Ground Kelp, Ground Giant Crab Meat and Breadcrumbs.
textByHash.db52bceec34098b966caaacab2a8b391e703d644: To start, you need to get some prime beef from a cow. The fields around Lumbridge are a great source. Cook it up - medium rare is perfect. Don't cook it for too long or you'll burn it...
textByHash.dc4581c5e1e5a02249da46fe7188f0f323039e00: Did you bring them? Remember, I need an eye of newt, a glass of greenman's ale, a rotten tomato, and a 'Dirty Blast'!
textByHash.de0c1991045dfdd20a2e64c78a3d79917235b791: Apparently the ash reacts with the freshly squeezed juice in the glass. It means you can't just buy one Blurberry made earlier, you'll have to make it yourself.
textByHash.decea7abc5a57c3a05e653c41e64c907a4ed61e5: You went to see his dad in the tunnels under white wolf mountain, and then got some rock cakes, if I remember rightly.
textByHash.e0ee397151206be6182a70718e2ea4cf7bc24f1b: That's easy, all you do is use a knife to slice the bread up into tiny, tiny chunks.
textByHash.e17eb56997cb2ca12818ea58a48413492b8420c1: They said 'Can you tell me any good jokes?' Got ya! [laugh][laugh][laugh]!
textByHash.e1879fc5afd39528b8991ac47688ce6d8b16c56c: Oh no! You told me it was really hard, and that you had to defeat a string of monsters without any prayer!
textByHash.e2f50a498e75d7650105b7bc1d70597d6d19ea00: Fine. I always knew you Adventurer types were callous beasts. Go on your merry way!
textByHash.e405783f10c929cde1235fe889984c0fc426f152: I don't have anything for you to do right now, sorry.
textByHash.e419081de43f0c8e1d465e6cfae004cd847e7415: Well, if you use a pestle and mortar on a raw cod that will grind it up nicely.
textByHash.e49bfbe545e95470ba851144851daf12f0a2f541: Well, I have always felt like a failure as a cook, and that my job here is down to the skills of my ancestor.
textByHash.e585d52f3b6889e76b382bf6dbe04f102d72848b: The minute I turn my back, he's caught red-handed trying to pickpocket one of our patrons. We gave him the boot there 'n' then.
textByHash.e5d877e6d37bc6c1eab2124c934385cf07d863ad: You'll need an empty bucket for the milk itself. The general store just north of the castle will sell you one for a couple of coins.
textByHash.e92b423cd93cbf3cfd3fc8d25f8177abdffd463b: I don't want to, that's why! Now, leave me alone.
textByHash.e9f225e22dec5cc92ad1ea6e6e08b85d63185ad8: I am in a terrible pickle, and I desperately need somebody trustworthy to help me out!
textByHash.eb88d431728d52062fcdcbe9989786d045fa0cfa: Tell me, do you know how to make a Fruit Blast?
textByHash.ebf824d4071b58599aad1b5ab96c49d9f6b37c4a: Is there anything else you need to know?
textByHash.ee58afe79f5cf431a8794a6db5d5194cdad7573a: That would be nice, but the Duke doesn't allow holidays for core staff.
textByHash.f072bd3182ce6fc13401ef2c8e9755d39fe8a259: You're not aristocracy but I suppose you'd do. Do you want a good cook for 3000 coins?
textByHash.f11739111c24739074e68fc636135248c9674a39: I've forgotten to buy the ingredients. I'll never get them in time now. He'll sack me! What will I do? I have four children and a goat to look after. Would you help me? Please?
textByHash.f13f34521b20d99c3a3a83dbe2d59650c42122d7: Chicken's lair, you came across a method of forcing a dragon to your will so that it would complete the dish, which you then used to protect Sir Amik!
textByHash.f1587c748ccdf6bf6cd873c53b83cacd974b6c1a: Ah, well, they're a popular Gnomic delicacy served at the Blurberry bar. But for this recipe I'd need a fresh one; you can't just buy me one from a shop or from your friends.
textByHash.f36e2a68cbb72b8c1572a7fb4a83df572d0a5caa: You were seriously great when you defeated the Culinaromancer! I can't believe I nearly caused all of those people to be killed! So how is the adventuring going now?
textByHash.f5bf8be0a26326ee65ff544b8b1a113d13fe3acf: Ha ha ha, you call this a sandwich!?
textByHash.f5dc71d5f30cd1e5c974b9f44b5b551c64cb16af: Come back and see me when you've got everything I need!
textByHash.f5f19febdde91456fb4508510b45f281c28db8a6: I'll have a look through my cookbook while I wait for the ingredients.
textByHash.f7367e791638d4686fe949ba9116127aecb2a46c: I simply won't take no for an answer!
textByHash.f9dc3213e78a6c3891111fbd95b68c9ad2afbd6c: I'm afraid not, only the big cheeses get to dine with the Duke.
textByHash.f9ec337da0087006fe6c00a1bb5bd54ffb98eceb: Yet you keep talking to me!
textByHash.fa0b61a92ea46b4d8ee31e8cfff5c04852b4f071: Its called the Cook-o-matic 100. It uses a combination of state of the art temperature regulation and magic.
textByHash.fa1e8d3e985713ca38719224683f04692895b726: How are you getting on with finding the ingredients?
textByHash.fbce5ee5ee86a0619116c85ee543b0a807f388c4: What do you want? I'm busy!
textByHash.fcc33e323dd5ac9ebffa1bd9a688ceb7624a76bc: Its all standard cook's issue uniform...
textByHash.fd5bc3886475dae066c50f5e3b439fcad1ce40aa: Alright. I can start work immediately.
